relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Saul still breathes threat against the Lord’s disciples.
He seeks authority to bind those of the Way.
Near Damascus, heaven-light interrupts the road.
The fallen persecutor hears his name twice.
Jesus identifies himself with the pursued community.
Saul must rise and be led into what comes next.
The companions hear but cannot behold the speaker.
Opened eyes see nothing; the binder is led by hand.
Three days of blindness and fasting become womb-darkness.
Ananias answers the vision: I am here.
Straight Street receives the praying Saul.
Saul has seen Ananias restoring sight by hands.
Ananias names the real harm Saul has done.
Saul’s authority to bind name-callers is known.
The Lord names him chosen vessel for name-bearing.
The persecutor will learn suffering for the name.
Ananias calls him brother and brings sight and Spirit.
Scales fall, sight returns, and baptism follows.
Food strengthens him into discipleship.
Immediately he proclaims Jesus as Son of the Divine.
Amazement rises: the ravager now invokes the name.
Saul is empowered and proves Jesus as Messiah.
The hunted one becomes hunted.
The plot at the gates becomes known.
Night and basket become the escape path.
Jerusalem fears the former persecutor.
Barnabas bridges Saul into the apostles through witness.
Saul moves openly in Jerusalem with bold name-speech.
Dispute again draws death-intent.
The siblings send Saul to Tarsus.
The assembly has peace, building, reverence, Spirit-comfort, and multiplication.
Peter moves among the holy ones to Lydda.
Aeneas has lain paralyzed eight years.
Jesus Messiah heals; Aeneas rises.
The sight of healing turns Lydda and Sharon to the Lord.
Tabitha/Dorcas is full of good works and mercy.
Her death brings washing and upper-room grief.
The disciples urgently call Peter.
Widows show the garments of her mercy.
Prayer, silence, and the command: Tabitha, rise.
Peter gives his hand and presents her alive.
Joppa hears, and many trust.
Peter remains with Simon the tanner, already near boundary-crossing.
